title: pfaffia dunaliana
intro: Pfaffia dunaliana, also known as Suma, is a plant species native to the grasslands of South America, specifically in the countries of Argentina, Brazil, and Paraguay. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant is rich in antioxidants and has anti-inflammatory properties, which contribute to its health benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health, boosting the immune system, and supporting liver function. Pfaffia dunaliana is often prepared as a tea or capsule, and it can also be consumed as a juice or added to soups and stews in traditional herbal preparations.
